transcript.clean_text: the snow-capped mountain of la molina waiting for the peloton at the end of 161 kilometers racing from uh perpignon heading inland and going uphill straight out of the back door for 78 kilometers slowly but surely getting more punishing before the first climb at mon luis and then color de jose and la molina itself at the end with so many mountains points up for grabs maximum of 30 available it was going to attempt to break away four brave souls went up the road to bertilli pedersen or kamika and bizkara sadly there were others that were really struggling on the day and tom dumoulin didn't want the cameras around him he wasn't well out of the race michael matthews looking equally as peaky we'd have to wait and see how he would fare throughout a day that was going to be tough so was the chase and the pace being set yet again by movistar that did so much damage to bike exchange the day prior simon yates having to battle his way back in and we were even worried about simon gates health at the end of the day he was the tail off the back on the final climb but when we got there there was just 12 kilometers remaining it's cara last man standing others were starting to roll the dice london abila from dsm set off in hot pursuit simon clark was doing his work and the man who owned all the jerseys at the beginning of the day said enough is enough weiderberg supreme yesterday but really in trouble as so many were on this incline bob is cara tagged by van der nabler but that was not the end of the story ben o'connor well always somebody who provides the unexpected he done it in the gyro he did it up to teen in the tour de france where he finished fourth last year and he was somebody to be wary of and everyone knew it the problem is they could not organize themselves into a fighting force to close down the gap in the end it was mark solaire that set off in hot pursuit but o'connor was hanging on so there looked like he got the drive when we headed to the little downhill section with three kilometers to go but the final kick up to the line the question was could oconna survive well ag2r citroen have offered him a four year contract and it's because of the guts that he has shown in grand tours he did it again a phenomenal performance dragging himself to the line making it three australian wins in a row after matthew's on the first day groves yesterday and now o'connor well it just left minor places for those chasing on quintana just edged by oyuzo at the line for uae team emirates some big names coming to the fore but none bigger on the day than o'connor absolutely remarkable performance timed it to perfection used all the skills he had most effectively o'connor uzo quintana igita in fourth ahead of almeida what polls looking good ahead of chicony johansson mata and house o'connor deserved everything he got today he got what was required and he used it o'connor takes the lead of the race by a margin of 10 seconds from iozo quintana only 12 seconds in arrears igita chicone this race is wide open as we head on for another big climbing day to boy taul on stage four 166.7 kilometers in total and three category one tests yet again another tough day in the dead center of this race well there will be some respite but it's a tough one the tour of catalunya the oldest race in spain will some of the old hands take a step forward it wasn't valverde today might it be tomorrow well there'll be plenty out to stop him we'll be here to find out how it goes you
